title: Illustrative Example 2 - Confidence Intervals for the Direct, Indirect, and Total Effects
text: object fit contains fitted dynr model data set sample size 133. See Fitting Continuous-Time Vector Autoregressive Model details.

what: Extract Elements of the Drift Matrix and the Process Noise Covariance Matrix
title: Illustrative Example 2 - Confidence Intervals for the Direct, Indirect, and Total Effects
text: extract elements drift matrix process noise covariance matrix corresponding sampling covariance matrix fit object.
code: # drift matrix phi_names <- c(   "phi_11",   "phi_21",   "phi_31",   "phi_12",   "phi_22",   "phi_32",   "phi_13",   "phi_23",   "phi_33" ) phi <- matrix(   data = coef(fit)[phi_names],   nrow = 3 ) # column names and row names are needed to define the indirect effects colnames(phi) <- rownames(phi) <- c(   "conflict",   "knowledge",   "competence" ) vcov_phi_vec <- vcov(fit)[phi_names, phi_names] # process noise covariance matrix sigma_names <- c(   "sigma_11",   "sigma_12",   "sigma_13",   "sigma_12",   "sigma_22",   "sigma_23",   "sigma_13",   "sigma_23",   "sigma_33" ) sigma <- matrix(   data = coef(fit)[sigma_names],   nrow = 3 ) # measurement error covariance matrix theta_names <- c(   "theta_11",   "theta_22",   "theta_33" ) theta <- diag(3) diag(theta) <- coef(fit)[theta_names] # combining the drift matrix and the process noise covariance matrix phi_sigma_names <- c(   "phi_11",   "phi_21",   "phi_31",   "phi_12",   "phi_22",   "phi_32",   "phi_13",   "phi_23",   "phi_33",   "sigma_11",   "sigma_12",   "sigma_13",   "sigma_22",   "sigma_23",   "sigma_33" ) vcov_theta <- matrix(   data = vcov(fit)[phi_sigma_names, phi_sigma_names],   nrow = length(phi_sigma_names) )

what: Delta Method Confidence Intervals For The Direct, Indirect, and Total Effects

text: Using DeltaMed function cTMed package, confidence intervals direct, indirect, total effects long sequence time interval values generated. makes regions significance visible. what: Parametric Bootstrap Method Confidence Intervals For The Direct, Indirect, and Total Effects
title: Illustrative Example 3 - Regions of Significance
text: parametric bootstrap approach involves generating data parametric model fitting model generated data multipke times. data generation model fitting hadled bootStateSpace package. estimated parameters passed arguments PBSSMOUFixed function bootStateSpace package, generates parametric bootstrap sampling distribution parameter estimates. argument R specifies number bootstrap replications. generated data model estimates stored path using specified prefix file names. ncores = parallel::detectCores() argument instructs function use available CPU cores system. NOTE: Fitting CT-VAR model multiple times computationally intensive. Using BootMed function cTMed package, confidence intervals direct, indirect, total effects long sequence time interval values generated. makes regions significance visible. Consider using ncores argument use multiple cores delta_t vector long. plot method BootMed presents regions significance visually represented shaded areas plot.
code: R <- 1000L path <- root$find_file(   ".setup",   "data-raw" ) prefix <- "pb_example_133" phi_hat <- phi sigma_hat <- sigma nu <- c(0, 0, 0) lambda <- diag(3) # fixed mu0 and sigma0 data("grundy2007", package = "manCTMed") data_0 <- grundy2007[which(grundy2007[, "time"] == 0), ] varnames <- c(   "conflict",   "knowledge",   "competence" ) mu0 <- colMeans(data_0)[varnames] sigma0 <- cov(data_0)[varnames, varnames] boot <- PBSSMOUFixed(   R = R,   path = path,   prefix = prefix,   n = 133,   time = 30,   delta_t = 0.10,   mu0 = mu0,   sigma0_l = t(chol(sigma0)),   mu = c(0, 0, 0),   phi = phi,   sigma_l = t(chol(sigma)),   nu = nu,   lambda = lambda,   theta_l = t(chol(theta)),   mu0_fixed = TRUE,   sigma0_fixed = TRUE,   ncores = parallel::detectCores(),   seed = 42 ) pb <- BootMed(   phi = extract(object = boot, what = "phi"), # extracts the bootstrap drift matrices   phi_hat = phi_hat,   delta_t = seq(from = 0, to = 10, length.out = 1000),   from = "conflict",   to = "competence",   med = "knowledge",   ncores = parallel::detectCores() ) plot(pb) plot(pb, type = "bc") # bias-corrected

title: Fitting the Continuous-Time Vector Autoregressive Model
code: data("grundy2007", package = "manCTMed")

title: Fitting the Continuous-Time Vector Autoregressive Model
code: dynr_data <- dynr::dynr.data(   dataframe = grundy2007,   id = "id",   time = "time",   observed = c(     "conflict",     "knowledge",     "competence"   ) )

title: Fitting the Continuous-Time Vector Autoregressive Model
code: data_0 <- data[which(data[, "time"] == 0), ] #> Error in data[, "time"]: object of type 'closure' is not subsettable dynr_initial <- dynr::prep.initial(   values.inistate = colMeans(data_0)[     c(       "conflict",       "knowledge",       "competence"     )   ],   params.inistate = rep(x = "fixed", times = 3),   values.inicov = cov(data_0)[     c(       "conflict",       "knowledge",       "competence"     ),     c(       "conflict",       "knowledge",       "competence"     )   ],   params.inicov = matrix(     data = "fixed",     nrow = 3,     ncol = 3   ) ) #> Error: object 'data_0' not found

title: Fitting the Continuous-Time Vector Autoregressive Model
code: dynr_measurement <- dynr::prep.measurement(   values.load = diag(3),   params.load = matrix(     data = "fixed",     nrow = 3,     ncol = 3   ),   state.names = paste0(     "eta_",     c(       "conflict",       "knowledge",       "competence"     )   ),   obs.names =     c(     "conflict",     "knowledge",     "competence"   ) )

title: Fitting the Continuous-Time Vector Autoregressive Model
code: dynr_dynamics <- dynr::prep.formulaDynamics(   formula = list(     eta_conflict ~ (phi_11 * eta_conflict) + (phi_12 * eta_knowledge) + (phi_13 * eta_competence),     eta_knowledge ~ (phi_21 * eta_conflict) + (phi_22 * eta_knowledge) + (phi_23 * eta_competence),     eta_competence ~ (phi_31 * eta_conflict) + (phi_32 * eta_knowledge) + (phi_33 * eta_competence)   ),   startval = c(     phi_11 = 0,     phi_12 = 0,     phi_13 = 0,     phi_21 = 0,     phi_22 = 0,     phi_23 = 0,     phi_31 = 0,     phi_32 = 0,     phi_33 = 0   ),   isContinuousTime = TRUE )

title: Fitting the Continuous-Time Vector Autoregressive Model
code: dynr_noise <- dynr::prep.noise(   values.latent = matrix(     data = c(       .10, .00, .00,       .00, .10, .00,       .00, .00, .10     ),     nrow = 3   ),   params.latent = matrix(     data = c(       "sigma_11", "sigma_12", "sigma_13",       "sigma_12", "sigma_22", "sigma_23",       "sigma_13", "sigma_23", "sigma_33"     ),     nrow = 3   ),   values.observed = matrix(     data = c(       .10, .00, .00,       .00, .10, .00,       .00, .00, .10     ),     nrow = 3   ),   params.observed = matrix(     data = c(       "theta_11", "fixed", "fixed",       "fixed", "theta_22", "fixed",       "fixed", "fixed", "theta_33"     ),     nrow = 3,     ncol = 3   ) )

title: Fitting the Continuous-Time Vector Autoregressive Model
code: model <- dynr::dynr.model(   data = dynr_data,   initial = dynr_initial,   measurement = dynr_measurement,   dynamics = dynr_dynamics,   noise = dynr_noise,   outfile = "ct-var.c" ) #> Error: object 'dynr_initial' not found

what: Add Model Constraints to Aid in Optimization

code: lb <- ub <- rep(NA, times = length(model$xstart)) #> Error: object 'model' not found names(ub) <- names(lb) <- names(model$xstart) #> Error: object 'model' not found lb[   c(     "phi_11",     "phi_21",     "phi_31",     "phi_12",     "phi_22",     "phi_32",     "phi_13",     "phi_23",     "phi_33"   ) ] <- -1.5 #> Error in rProject::VignettesPrecompile("/scratch/ibp5092/manCTMed"): object 'lb' not found ub[   c(     "phi_11",     "phi_21",     "phi_31",     "phi_12",     "phi_22",     "phi_32",     "phi_13",     "phi_23",     "phi_33"   ) ] <- 1.5 #> Error in rProject::VignettesPrecompile("/scratch/ibp5092/manCTMed"): object 'ub' not found ub[   c(     "phi_11",     "phi_22",     "phi_33"   ) ] <- 0 #> Error in rProject::VignettesPrecompile("/scratch/ibp5092/manCTMed"): object 'ub' not found lb[   c(     "sigma_11",     "sigma_22",     "sigma_33"   ) ] <- .Machine$double.xmin #> Error in rProject::VignettesPrecompile("/scratch/ibp5092/manCTMed"): object 'lb' not found lb[   c(     "theta_11",     "theta_22",     "theta_33"   ) ] <- .Machine$double.xmin #> Error in rProject::VignettesPrecompile("/scratch/ibp5092/manCTMed"): object 'lb' not found model$lb <- lb #> Error: object 'lb' not found model$ub <- ub #> Error: object 'ub' not found
